slide3.title: Y.K.N Yvette Kapinga Ngandu
slide3.text: Ambassador Yvette Kapinga Ngandu was appointed Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of the DRC to the United States in June 2025 and assumed her duties on October 30, 2025. Prior to this appointment, Ambassador Ngandu served as Commissioner for Gender Promotion and Human and Social Development at the Economic Community of Central African States (ECCAS) Commission from August 30, 2020, to August 2025, with her duty station in Libreville, Gabon. In this capacity, she became the first woman to head the largest department of ECCAS, overseeing ten key sectors....

history.title: History of relations between DRC and the United States

0.title: Rumble in the Jungle (1974): Ali vs. Foreman in Kinshasa
0.excerpt: The legendary fight between Muhammad Ali and George Foreman in Kinshasa has become a global cultural symbol and a major moment in sports history.

1.title: Central African roots and contribution to American history
1.excerpt: Part of the ancestors of African Americans comes from Central Africa (Kongo/Angola region). These communities contributed to the economic and cultural development of the United States.

2.title: DRC – United States relations: diplomacy and cooperation since 1960
2.excerpt: The United States established diplomatic relations with the DRC in 1960, at independence, with the opening of an embassy in Léopoldville (Kinshasa).

ambassador.photoAlt: Ambassador of DRC to the United States
ambassador.title: Ambassador
ambassador.role: Ambassador of the Democratic Republic of Congo to the United States of America

paragraphs.p1: Her Excellency Ms. Yvette Ngandu Kapinga was appointed Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of the Democratic Republic of Congo to the United States of America in June 2025. She officially assumed her duties in Washington, D.C., on October 30, 2025.
paragraphs.p2: Before this appointment, the Ambassador held, since August 30, 2020, the position of Commissioner for Gender Promotion, Human and Social Development within the Commission of the Economic Community of Central African States (ECCAS), based in Libreville, Gabon, a position she held until August 2025.
paragraphs.p3: In this capacity, she was the first woman to lead the largest department of ECCAS, covering ten essential sectors, namely: health, humanitarian action, education, scientific research, culture, migration, employment, youth, sport, gender and social affairs. She was also the youngest of the seven elected members of the new Commission, at only 44 years old.
paragraphs.p4: Ambassador Ngandu has over 25 years of professional experience in diplomatic and international institutions, with recognized expertise in mediation, conflict prevention and resolution.

institutions: Economic Community of Central African States (ECCAS)
African Union Commission, Addis Ababa (12 years)
United States Department of State, Washington, D.C.
ambassador.contributionsParagraph: At the continental level, her major contributions include her active participation in drafting the African Union Declaration on Gender (adopted in July 2004), as well as the African Union Report on Women and Children in Armed Conflict (2010). These works contributed, in June 2017, to the creation of the Pan-African Network of Women Mediators (FemWise-Africa).
ambassador.mediationParagraph: In 2008, she founded and led the Continental Mediation Unit and the Secretariat of the Panel of the Wise of the African Union, which she coordinated for twelve years. In this strategic position, she led numerous preventive diplomacy and good offices missions, notably:
mediationList: Post-election mediation led by Kofi Annan in Kenya (2008)
Processes in Tunisia and Egypt (2011–2013) during the Arab Spring
Good offices of Edem Kodjo in the DRC (2016–2018)
Interventions in Senegal, Ghana, Sierra Leone, Togo and Burkina Faso
ambassador.reportsParagraph: Under the leadership of several Presidents of the African Union Commission — Jean Ping, Nkosazana Dlamini Zuma and Moussa Faki Mahamat — Ambassador Ngandu supervised the drafting of major thematic reports on peace and security in Africa, including:
reportsList: Women and children in armed conflicts (2010)
Promoting a culture of peace in Africa (2015)
ambassador.trainingParagraph: Before her involvement with the African Union, she served as Senior Director of Africa Programs at the International Peace Institute (IPI) in New York (2003–2008). She also collaborated with the National Endowment for Democracy (NED), Voice of America and the U.S. Department of State in Washington, D.C.
ambassador.trainingParagraph2: Holding a Master's degree in Public Administration and International Relations from Bowling Green State University (Ohio, USA), Her Excellency Yvette Ngandu Kapinga has participated in more than thirty African Union Summits as well as countless seminars and peace missions across the African continent, making her a recognized expert in African affairs.
ambassador.languagesParagraph: A polyglot, she speaks French, English, Spanish and Lingala fluently, and has a good command of Portuguese and Swahili.
ambassador.responsibilitiesParagraph: She also sits on the Board of Directors of the Center for Peace and Conflict Resolution in Africa at California State University, Sacramento, as well as on the Technical Advisory Committee of the Institute for Peace and Security Studies (IPSS) in Addis Ababa.
